findings.details: The methodology constants pulled from the Trends page source contain three unresolved placeholders: '2026-05-XX' (Added llms.txt), '2026-05-YY' (Added FAQ JSON-LD schema), and '2026-05-ZZ' (Updated robots.txt for AI crawlers), each tagged '# FIXME: fill in actual ship date'. These render to users as literal XX/YY/ZZ dates and undermine credibility of the AI readiness section.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 2026-05-XX/YY/ZZ with the actual ship dates from git history or deploy logs for llms.txt, FAQ JSON-LD, and the robots.txt AI-crawler update, and remove the FIXME comments.
findings.title: AI readiness milestones still contain FIXME placeholders

findings.details: Post 06 ('50 Questions to Ask Your Parents Before It's Too Late') has publish_by 2026-06-21 and event_date 2026-06-21 — same day. Google needs days-to-weeks to index and rank seasonal content; publishing on the event date guarantees the post misses the Father's Day search wave entirely. Compare Post 01 which correctly has a ~7-week lead time for the same event.
findings.suggested_fix: Move Post 06 publish_by back to at least 2026-05-31 (3 weeks of indexing lead time before June 21), or earlier to match the lead time used for Post 01.
findings.title: Post 06 publishes on Father's Day itself — zero indexing lead time

findings.details: Both archived items (Post 02 and the Mother's Day cluster) had deadline 2026-05-10 but swept_at 2026-05-16 — meaning they sat on the active dashboard as 'overdue Mother's Day' content for 6 days after Mother's Day had already passed. The sweep should fire on deadline+1. This suggests the auto-archive job is running weekly (or manually) rather than daily.
findings.suggested_fix: Audit the auto-archive cron — it should run daily at 00:00 local and sweep anything with deadline < today. Verify it's not gated on completion status (these were Incomplete and still needed to archive on the date basis).
findings.title: Sweep ran 6 days after Mother's Day deadline

findings.details: Post 03 has publish_by 2026-05-10 and event_date null (evergreen commercial-intent). Today is 2026-05-16 so it's 6 days overdue, but unlike Post 01/06 there is no future event to justify the overdue red border — it's just late evergreen work. The 'pre-event signal' exemption in the note doesn't apply here.
findings.suggested_fix: Either ship Post 03 immediately or reset publish_by to a realistic new date. If consistently slipping, deprioritize behind the Father's Day SOON-window posts which have hard event deadlines.
findings.title: Post 03 (Remento alternative) is 6 days past publish_by with no event anchor
